hypothesis: The prepared Q0.24 top-k comparator can be made more robust for the first Verilator pass by eliminating width-sensitive literal and multiply forms while preserving the existing expected-state oracle. This is useful under the current no-board, tool-blocked slice because it reduces the chance that the next recovered simulator run spends time on avoidable width/literal warnings rather than the comparator behavior itself.
changes: Changed rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare.sv SCORE_MIN from a 46-bit hex literal to {1'b1, 45'd0}.
Added explicit 47-bit signed accumulator and scale extensions before the Q0.24 multiply in rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare.sv.
Added a matching SCORE_MIN localparam in rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb.sv for the reserved-sidecar reject expectation.

commands.cmd: sed reads of h2-int8-l2-selftest-board-comparison.json, h2-v4k-scale-up-summary.json, and h2-vocab-memory-surface-score.json

metrics.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

decision.rationale: The comparator behavior surface and test vectors are unchanged, but the RTL now avoids a width-sensitive signed multiply expression and the testbench no longer relies on a non-nibble-aligned hex signed-min literal. This is not simulator PASS evidence because executable validation remains blocked before tool startup.
decision.next_gate: Recover command execution, then run: verilator --binary --timing -Wall rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare.sv rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb.sv && ./obj_dir/Vtask6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb. If it passes, emit h2-q024-topk-comparator-cutout-result.json. If it fails, fix only this tiny comparator/testbench before any larger scorecard.

next_gate: First simulator validation of the hardened Q0.24 comparator cutout; no DDR3 integration, full synthesis, or hardware programming.
